frameworks.name: prefrontal cortex basal ganglia working memory model (PBWM)
frameworks.one_line: gating in basal ganglia regulates updating vs maintenance of WM contents

frameworks.name: phasic striatal dopamine signalling
frameworks.one_line: ebEBR is used as a behavioural proxy for phasic striatal dopamine activity

core_question: Can event-based eye-blink rate dissociate working-memory updating from gate switching in real time?
key_assumptions: event-based eye-blink rate reflects phasic striatal dopamine activity
gating and updating are separable cognitive processes
the reference-back task independently manipulates updating and gate switching
manipulations: trial-by-trial demands on WM updating vs maintenance
trial-by-trial demands on gate switching vs gate repetition
key_findings: ebEBR increases on trials requiring WM updating
ebEBR increases on trials requiring gate switching
the updating and gate-switching effects replicate across three experiments
gate opening appears more stimulus-driven and retroactive than gate closing
interpretations: results support PBWM by linking updating and gating to striatal dopaminergic activity
switching to maintenance mode is more context-driven than switching to updating mode
